title: Owlvex Curated Framework Pack 2026.1
description: Curated framework catalog and prompt-oriented guidance derived from official upstream sources and Owlvex-authored interpretations.

frameworks.name: OWASP Top 10
frameworks.description: Developer-facing application security risk framing used for AI prioritization, output vocabulary, and issue mapping.
frameworks.license_notes: Official OWASP project content; use curated derivatives in prompts rather than dumping raw project text.

frameworks.name: OWASP API Security Top 10
frameworks.description: API-specific risk framing for uncovered AI findings and remediation language around API misuse and authorization boundaries.
frameworks.license_notes: Official OWASP API Security content is CC BY-SA; use compact curated summaries and mappings.

frameworks.name: Common Weakness Enumeration
frameworks.description: Weakness taxonomy backbone used for canonical issue identity and stable mapping output.
frameworks.license_notes: Official MITRE export mirrored locally for curation and lookup.

frameworks.name: MITRE ATT&CK
frameworks.description: Attacker behavior and technique mapping used as a secondary context layer for exploit path framing.
frameworks.license_notes: Official STIX data mirrored locally; prompts should use compact technique references, not the full bundle.

frameworks.name: NIST SP 800-53
frameworks.description: Control-set mapping layer for policy and compliance-oriented interpretation of security findings.
frameworks.license_notes: Official derivative spreadsheet mirrored locally for curated control mapping use.

frameworks.name: HIPAA Security Rule
frameworks.description: Healthcare security and privacy compliance lens used as a curated reference profile pending a more complete mirrored source import.
frameworks.license_notes: Currently reference-backed only in this repo because automated fetch was blocked.

frameworks.name: PCI DSS
frameworks.description: Payment security compliance lens used for curated requirement mapping and stricter handling of credential, logging, and segmentation issues.
frameworks.license_notes: Use Owlvex-authored summaries and requirement identifiers rather than mirroring standard text wholesale.

frameworks.name: Clean Code
frameworks.description: Owlvex-authored code quality lens for maintainability, readability, and defensive engineering hygiene.
frameworks.license_notes: Owlvex-curated profile; no single upstream canonical dataset.

profiles.title: OWASP Top 10 Risk Framing
profiles.purpose: Use OWASP as a concise application security lens for AI-only findings and report phrasing.
prompt_guidance: Prefer OWASP category labels only when the code evidence supports the mapped risk area.
Use OWASP to explain the class of weakness, not as proof by itself.
Keep remediation practical and developer-facing rather than compliance-heavy.
ai_usage_rules: OWASP may shape vocabulary and prioritization more strongly for AI findings than for deterministic findings.
Do not widen severity just because a weakness falls under a prominent OWASP category.

profiles.title: OWASP API Security Risk Framing
profiles.purpose: Focus AI findings on API misuse, object-level authorization, and API-specific exposure patterns.
prompt_guidance: Prefer API-specific labels when the code or endpoint behavior is clearly API-facing.
Emphasize object-level authorization, authentication, inventory, and excessive data exposure where relevant.
Avoid forcing API Top 10 labels onto generic non-API code.
ai_usage_rules: Use API OWASP for uncovered AI-only issues when endpoint semantics matter.
Do not emit API OWASP mappings for clearly non-API local code unless the surrounding context justifies it.

profiles.title: CWE Weakness Backbone
profiles.purpose: Use CWE as the primary weakness taxonomy for stable canonical issue identity and mapping.
prompt_guidance: Resolve AI findings to the closest precise CWE when the code evidence supports it.
Prefer no CWE mapping over a weak or speculative one.
Use CWE identifiers to stabilize cross-run comparison and issue normalization.
ai_usage_rules: CWE should be the strongest structured taxonomy field for AI findings.
If AI emits malformed CWE values, Owlvex should fall back to curated canonical mappings.

profiles.title: MITRE ATT&CK Adversary Context
profiles.purpose: Use ATT&CK as a secondary attacker-behavior lens, not as the primary proof of a code weakness.
prompt_guidance: Use ATT&CK only when the exploit path or technique mapping is reasonably clear from the code.
Prefer compact technique identifiers and names over long tactic narratives.
Do not use ATT&CK to inflate certainty for code-local findings.
ai_usage_rules: ATT&CK should enrich exploit context, not replace weakness identification.
Leave ATT&CK empty when the code alone does not support a believable technique correlation.

profiles.title: NIST SP 800-53 Control Mapping
profiles.purpose: Use NIST as a control-set interpretation layer for policy and compliance-aware reporting.
prompt_guidance: Use NIST mappings to support governance and control language, not to prove the finding exists.
Prefer specific control IDs where the mapping is already curated in Owlvex issue data.
Keep NIST output concise and avoid turning developer reports into audit prose.
ai_usage_rules: NIST should be secondary to code-grounded explanation and remediation.
If NIST mappings are absent or weak, fall back to canonical issue mappings rather than generating speculative controls.

profiles.title: HIPAA Security Rule Reference Lens
profiles.purpose: Use HIPAA as a narrow healthcare compliance lens for data protection, access control, and auditability findings.
prompt_guidance: Treat HIPAA as a compliance interpretation layer, especially around protected health information handling.
Use HIPAA language sparingly and only when the code context plausibly touches healthcare data handling.
Prefer broader security explanations first, then add HIPAA relevance as a secondary note.
ai_usage_rules: HIPAA is currently a curated reference profile in Owlvex, not a fully mirrored dataset.
Do not present HIPAA output as exhaustive legal compliance analysis.

profiles.title: PCI DSS Reference Lens
profiles.purpose: Use PCI DSS as a payment security interpretation layer for credential handling, logging, segmentation, and hardening issues.
prompt_guidance: Use PCI DSS only as a secondary requirement lens when the code context relates to payment processing or cardholder data handling.
Prefer requirement identifiers or concise control implications over long standard prose.
Keep remediation code-first; treat PCI language as supporting context.
ai_usage_rules: PCI DSS is currently a curated reference profile in Owlvex, not a fully mirrored standard text pack.
Do not imply formal PCI compliance certification from code-local findings.

profiles.title: Owlvex Clean Code Profile
profiles.purpose: Use a curated engineering-quality lens for maintainability, readability, and safer implementation hygiene.
prompt_guidance: Use Clean Code to explain maintainability and defensive engineering improvements distinctly from security proof.
Do not suppress real security findings just because Clean Code is selected alone.
Prefer precise code-change advice over abstract style criticism.
ai_usage_rules: Clean Code should change explanation style more than finding truth.
Use it as an adjacent quality lens, not as a substitute security taxonomy.
